title: olearia pachyphylla
intro: Olearia pachyphylla, also known as the bushy tree daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to eastern Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Olearia pachyphylla can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
